Title: Analysis of the Coptis chinensis genome reveals the diversification of protoberberine-type alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream branch and late protoberberine diversification steps
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes methylenedioxy bridge formation on multiple protoberberine intermediates, converting (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine to (S)-canadine, likely (S)-scoulerine to nandinine/cheilanthifoline-related product, and (S)-cheilanthifoline to (S)-stylopine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms pathway intermediates leading to berberine and coptisine-related alkaloids; contributes to diversification of protoberberines
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step not formally established; described as a key CYP719 gene with functional versatility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis/diversification of protoberberine-type alkaloids by functioning as a versatile methylenedioxy bridge-forming enzyme in berberine and coptisine-related pathways
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive compounds with reported anti-infective, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular-, diabetes-, cancer- and nervous system-related benefits
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NADPH-CYP reductase in yeast assay system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative phylogeny; tissue expression profiling; qRT-PCR; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae WAT11; microsomal in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS/QTOF metabolite identification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq, metabolomic profiling (BioProject PRJNA662860)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to enhance or diversify protoberberine alkaloid production; multifunctionality suggests utility for pathway compression but also cautions about product promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only among the three tested CYP719 genes did Cch00017825 accept all three substrates. It clustered with CjCYP719A1/CAS and showed strong homology to CAS, SPS and CFS genes, indicating unusual catalytic versatility. The paper highlights this gene as a key driver of alkaloid diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cch00017825 is the major actively expressed CYP719 gene, especially in rhizomes. 2. It is phylogenetically closest to CjCYP719A1 and is proposed to encode a versatile methylenedioxy bridge-forming enzyme. 3. Recombinant enzyme assays showed conversion of (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine to (S)-canadine, (S)-cheilanthifoline to (S)-stylopine, and acceptance of (S)-scoulerine, supporting a central role in protoberberine diversification.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative CYP719 enzyme, but no catalytic activity was detected with the tested substrates in yeast microsomal assays
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Proposed candidate for alkaloid diversification but not supported for tested reactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No demonstrated catalytic contribution to the tested protoberberine alkaloid conversions
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NADPH-CYP reductase in yeast assay system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae WAT11; microsomal in vitro assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited current value because no activity was shown for tested substrates
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Clustered with CjCYP719A18 in phylogeny; one of the tandemly duplicated CYP719 genes on chromosome 3.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cch00017817 was selected as a candidate CYP719 for biochemical testing. 2. Recombinant protein showed no activity toward three tested alkaloid substrates. 3. Its role in protoberberine diversification remains unresolved.
